steps.title: Use the naming rule
steps.content: The sentence explains that no two minor planets may share the same name. Therefore, any chosen name must belong to only one body and not be usable for another.

steps.title: Reject non-unique meanings
steps.content: Choice B means suggestive of something, not unique to it. Choice C means typical, which does not prevent duplicate names. Choice D means replaced by, which makes no sense for a name and a body. The answer is A.
